_meta.title: RWA Tokenization Workflow Template — Complete Governance Framework
_meta.author: PleoChrome Operations
_meta.description: Exhaustive stage/task/subtask breakdown for tokenizing real-world assets across four asset classes: gemstones, precious metals, real estate, and mineral rights. US regulatory framework. Designed to seed governance_requirements, default_tasks, and governance_documents tables.
_meta.regulatory_framework: United States (SEC, FINRA, FinCEN, state blue sky laws)
notes: Each stage maps to a workflow_phase enum in the PleoChrome schema.
Tasks map to governance_requirements rows; subtasks map to default_tasks rows.
Regulatory citations reference specific US federal and state laws.
Duration estimates assume a single asset with no blocking issues.
All three value paths share Stages 1-4; Stages 5-6 diverge by path.

stages.description: Initial outreach, qualification, and pipeline entry. The goal is to identify high-value assets, verify holder identity, assess feasibility, and decide whether to engage.

gate_at_end.gate_description: Asset holder verified. KYC/KYB passed. Provenance (or title/assay) documented. Engagement agreement signed. Deal committee Go decision.
required_to_pass: KYC/KYB clearance on asset holder
Signed NDA
Preliminary provenance/title/assay documentation collected
Preliminary valuation within target range
Internal deal committee Go decision

tasks.title: Identify Target Asset
tasks.description: Source asset from dealer networks, vault inventories, broker introductions, direct holder outreach, or inbound inquiries. Confirm minimum value threshold, asset type eligibility, and holder willingness to engage.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Securities offerings require sufficient asset value to justify compliance costs. Minimum thresholds ensure economic viability of the offering structure.

asset_class_variations.gemstones: Source from vault inventories, dealer networks, auction houses, private collections. Minimum $1M. Verify species, origin, and existing certifications.
asset_class_variations.precious_metals: Source LBMA Good Delivery bars, allocated bullion, coin collections, or mining output. Minimum $500K. Verify purity, weight, hallmarks, and LBMA chain of custody.
asset_class_variations.real_estate: Source commercial/residential properties, land parcels, development projects. Minimum $1M. Verify clear title, zoning, existing encumbrances, and cash flow history.
asset_class_variations.mineral_rights: Source producing or proven mineral interests (oil, gas, hard minerals). Minimum $500K. Verify lease status, production history, reserve estimates, and operator relationships.

subtasks.title: Research asset pipeline sources
subtasks.description: Identify and catalog potential asset sources: broker networks, vault inventories, auction records, direct holder outreach lists, inbound lead forms.

subtasks.title: Screen for minimum value threshold
subtasks.description: Validate that the asset meets minimum value requirements ($500K-$1M+ depending on class) to justify compliance expenditure.

subtasks.title: Assess asset type eligibility
subtasks.description: Confirm the asset class is within PleoChrome's scope and does not present unresolvable regulatory barriers (e.g., sanctioned origin, contested title).

subtasks.title: Log lead in CRM pipeline
subtasks.description: Create asset record in CRM with status 'prospect'. Assign reference code. Tag asset class and preliminary value path.

tasks.title: Holder Introduction & NDA
tasks.description: First contact with asset holder or their representative. Execute mutual NDA to protect confidential information. Share platform overview and assess holder sophistication and expectations.
tasks.regulatory_basis: NDAs protect trade secrets and material non-public information during evaluation. Required under Defend Trade Secrets Act and to preserve confidentiality of offering structure details.

subtasks.title: Schedule introductory meeting/call
subtasks.description: Arrange first contact with asset holder. In-person preferred for high-value assets; video for initial screen.

subtasks.title: Prepare and send mutual NDA
subtasks.description: Draft or customize mutual NDA from template. Send to holder for review and execution.

subtasks.title: Execute NDA (both parties sign)
subtasks.description: Obtain fully executed NDA with wet or electronic signatures from both parties.

subtasks.title: Deliver platform overview deck
subtasks.description: Share PleoChrome overview deck covering value paths, process, timeline, fees, and competitive advantages.

subtasks.title: Assess holder sophistication
subtasks.description: Evaluate holder's understanding of securities, tokenization, timelines, and fee structures. Note any red flags or education needs.

tasks.title: KYC / KYB on Asset Holder
tasks.description: Full identity verification on the individual or entity. OFAC/SDN screening. PEP check. Adverse media scan. For entities: verify beneficial ownership (all owners >25%).
tasks.regulatory_basis: Bank Secrecy Act and FinCEN regulations require identification and verification of all parties in financial transactions. OFAC sanctions compliance is mandatory — transacting with SDN-listed persons is a federal crime. Dealers in Precious Metals, Stones, or Jewels (DPMS) have heightened AML obligations under FinCEN.
tasks.regulatory_citation: BSA 31 USC 5311-5332; FinCEN 31 CFR Part 1027; OFAC 31 CFR Part 501; CDD Rule 31 CFR 1010.230

subtasks.title: Collect government-issued ID (individuals)
subtasks.description: Obtain passport or driver's license. Verify identity against government databases.

subtasks.title: Collect entity formation documents (entities)
subtasks.description: Obtain articles of incorporation/organization, operating agreement, certificate of good standing.

subtasks.title: Verify beneficial ownership (>25% owners)
subtasks.description: Identify all beneficial owners per CDD Rule. Collect ID for each. Verify against corporate records.

subtasks.title: Run OFAC/SDN screening
subtasks.description: Screen all individuals and entities against OFAC Specially Designated Nationals list, EU sanctions lists, and other applicable sanctions databases.

subtasks.title: Run PEP (Politically Exposed Person) check
subtasks.description: Screen against global PEP databases. Flag any matches for enhanced due diligence.

subtasks.title: Run adverse media scan
subtasks.description: Search news databases and public records for negative coverage, litigation, fraud allegations, or regulatory actions.

subtasks.title: Compliance officer review and sign-off
subtasks.description: Compliance officer reviews all KYC/KYB results and issues clearance or flags for enhanced review.

tasks.title: Provenance / Title / Rights Verification
tasks.description: Collect and verify the complete chain of custody, ownership history, or rights documentation for the asset. This is the foundational evidence that the holder has clear, unencumbered rights to the asset.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Provenance establishes clean title and regulatory compliance. Required to protect against stolen property claims, title disputes, and fraud. For gemstones, Kimberley Process Certification is required for diamond trade. For real estate, clear title is a precondition to any securities offering.
tasks.regulatory_citation: Clean Diamond Trade Act 19 U.S.C. 3901-3913; UCC 2-403 (good faith purchaser); State recording statutes

gemstones.description: Collect complete chain of custody: mine/origin to dealer to current holder. Verify Kimberley Process compliance if applicable (diamonds). Flag any gaps in provenance chain.
specific_subtasks: Collect mine-of-origin documentation or dealer acquisition records
Verify Kimberley Process Certificate (diamonds only)
Trace chain of custody through each dealer/auction/private sale
Identify and document any provenance gaps
Verify no theft reports or restitution claims exist

precious_metals.description: Verify LBMA chain of custody for gold/silver bars. Confirm hallmarks, refiner marks, and serial numbers. For coins, verify mint records and collector provenance.
specific_subtasks: Verify LBMA Good Delivery status and refiner hallmarks
Confirm bar serial numbers against refiner records
Obtain assay certificates from LBMA-accredited assayer
Verify chain of custody from refiner to current holder
Check against stolen bullion databases

real_estate.description: Order preliminary title report. Verify ownership chain. Identify all liens, encumbrances, easements, and restrictions. Confirm zoning and permitted use.
specific_subtasks: Order preliminary title report from title company
Review title chain for completeness and gaps
Identify all liens, mortgages, and encumbrances
Review easements, covenants, and use restrictions
Verify zoning classification and permitted use
Obtain survey (ALTA/NSPS if available)
Check for environmental liens or contamination (Phase I ESA reference)
Verify property tax status (current, delinquent, or tax sale)

mineral_rights.description: Verify mineral rights ownership separate from surface rights. Confirm lease status, royalty obligations, and operator agreements. Run title opinion.
specific_subtasks: Obtain mineral rights title opinion from landman or attorney
Verify severance of mineral rights from surface estate
Review all active leases and royalty agreements
Confirm operator agreements and working interest percentages
Verify no outstanding disputes or litigation on mineral rights
Obtain production history from state oil/gas commission
Review division orders for accuracy
Check for overriding royalty interests (ORRI) or net profits interests

tasks.title: Existing Certification / Appraisal Review
tasks.description: Collect and verify any existing certifications, appraisals, inspections, or valuation reports. Assess whether re-certification or new appraisals are needed based on report age, scope, and credibility.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Independent certification establishes the physical properties and value basis of the asset. Stale or unverified reports cannot support an offering valuation. Verification against issuing authority databases prevents fraud.

asset_class_variations.gemstones: Collect prior GIA/SSEF/Gubelin reports. Verify report numbers via GIA Report Check. Assess if re-certification needed (reports >2 years old or incomplete scope).
asset_class_variations.precious_metals: Collect assay certificates, refiner certificates, LBMA Good Delivery certificates. Verify serial numbers and hallmarks against refiner databases.
asset_class_variations.real_estate: Collect prior appraisals (MAI/SRA designated). Collect recent inspection reports, environmental assessments (Phase I ESA), and any engineering studies. Assess staleness (>6 months may require update).
asset_class_variations.mineral_rights: Collect reserve reports (SEC-compliant or PRMS-standard), decline curve analyses, production data from state agencies. Assess report currency and whether reserve estimates need updating.

tasks.title: Preliminary Valuation Estimate
tasks.description: Desktop valuation using comparable sales, market data, and existing appraisals. This is an internal screening tool — NOT the offering value. Used to assess whether the asset justifies compliance expenditure.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Preliminary valuation is required for internal deal committee review and to establish whether the asset justifies the compliance expenditure. Must be clearly distinguished from formal USPAP/independent appraisal process.

subtasks.title: Gather comparable market data
subtasks.description: Research recent comparable sales, auction results, market indices, and dealer price lists relevant to the asset class.

subtasks.title: Prepare preliminary valuation memo
subtasks.description: Draft internal memo with valuation range, methodology, comparables used, and confidence level. Include asset class-specific considerations.

subtasks.title: Calculate estimated offering economics
subtasks.description: Model projected offering size, fee revenue, compliance costs, and timeline to assess deal viability.

asset_class_variations.gemstones: Use auction records (Christie's, Sotheby's), dealer price lists (Rapaport for diamonds), and prior appraisals. Account for rarity premiums and market trends.
asset_class_variations.precious_metals: Use LBMA fix prices, COMEX futures, numismatic premium guides (for coins). Straightforward for standard bullion; complex for rare coins or historical pieces.
asset_class_variations.real_estate: Use comparable sales (MLS data, CoStar), cap rate analysis, income approach (NOI/cap rate), and replacement cost approach. Account for location, condition, and market trends.
asset_class_variations.mineral_rights: Use production data, commodity price forecasts, decline curve analysis, PV-10 calculation, and comparable mineral rights transactions. Account for reserve risk and commodity price volatility.

tasks.title: Engagement Agreement
tasks.description: Execute formal engagement agreement with the asset holder. Defines fee structure, timeline expectations, mutual obligations, representations and warranties regarding asset title and encumbrances, and termination provisions.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Written engagement agreement establishes the legal relationship, defines scope, allocates risk, and provides contractual basis for fee collection. Required for professional liability insurance and investor confidence.

subtasks.title: Draft engagement agreement from template
subtasks.description: Customize engagement agreement template for asset class, value path, and deal-specific terms.

subtasks.title: Internal legal review
subtasks.description: General counsel reviews engagement terms, fee structure, indemnification provisions, and termination clauses.

subtasks.title: Negotiate terms with holder
subtasks.description: Address holder feedback on fees, timeline, exclusivity, and other material terms.

subtasks.title: Execute engagement agreement (both parties sign)
subtasks.description: Obtain fully executed agreement with wet or electronic signatures from both parties.

tasks.title: Internal Deal Committee Review
tasks.description: Founders and senior team review the complete deal package. Go/No-Go decision based on provenance quality, valuation potential, holder reliability, regulatory risk, compliance cost estimate, and strategic fit.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Internal deal committee review is a SOC 2 processing integrity control and securities compliance best practice. Documented decision-making provides evidence of reasonable judgment and protects against liability.

subtasks.title: Prepare deal committee package
subtasks.description: Compile all intake documents: KYC results, provenance docs, preliminary valuation, engagement terms, risk assessment, and recommended value path.

subtasks.title: Schedule and conduct deal committee meeting
subtasks.description: Present deal to CEO, CTO, and CRO. Discuss risks, economics, timeline, and strategic fit.

subtasks.title: Record Go/No-Go decision with rationale
subtasks.description: Document the decision, rationale, conditions (if any), and dissenting views in the decision audit log.

subtasks.title: Notify holder of decision
subtasks.description: Communicate Go decision and next steps to holder, or gracefully decline with No-Go rationale.

stages.description: Comprehensive asset due diligence, independent certification, and formal valuation. The goal is to establish an independently verified, defensible asset value that can support a securities offering.

gate_at_end.gate_description: All independent certifications received. Formal appraisals complete. Variance within threshold. Offering value locked. No unresolved red flags.
required_to_pass: Independent lab/inspection reports received and verified
All formal appraisals complete (minimum 2, target 3)
Appraisal variance within 15-20% threshold
Offering value determined and locked
All provenance/title/rights issues resolved or documented

tasks.title: Independent Lab Certification / Inspection
tasks.description: Submit asset for independent verification by a recognized authority. This establishes the physical properties, authenticity, and condition of the asset independent of any party's claims.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Independent certification from a recognized authority is essential for establishing the physical characteristics and authenticity of the asset. Required for institutional investor confidence, insurance underwriting, and regulatory defensibility.

gemstones.description: Submit to GIA laboratory for grading: species identification, color grade, clarity, cut, carat weight, and origin determination.
specific_subtasks: Package stone per GIA submission requirements
Arrange insured transit to GIA laboratory (Carlsbad or New York)
Track submission and respond to any lab queries
Receive and verify GIA report
Verify report number via GIA Report Check online
If needed: submit to SSEF or Gubelin for supplemental origin determination ($0-5K, 2 weeks additional)

precious_metals.description: Submit to LBMA-accredited assayer for purity testing, weight verification, and hallmark confirmation. For coins: submit to PCGS or NGC for grading and authentication.
specific_subtasks: Select LBMA-accredited assayer or numismatic grading service
Arrange insured transit to assay facility
Conduct XRF or fire assay for purity verification
Verify weight against hallmark/documentation
Receive assay certificate with serial number cross-reference
For coins: obtain PCGS/NGC grading and encapsulation

real_estate.description: Order Phase I Environmental Site Assessment (ESA), property condition report, ALTA survey, and any specialized inspections (structural, MEP, roof, seismic).
specific_subtasks: Engage environmental consultant for Phase I ESA (ASTM E1527-21)
Engage property inspector for condition assessment
Order ALTA/NSPS land survey
Order specialized inspections as needed (structural, seismic, roof, MEP, ADA)
Review inspection reports and identify deficiencies
Determine if Phase II ESA is warranted based on Phase I findings
Compile inspection summary with cost-to-cure estimates

mineral_rights.description: Commission independent reserve report from qualified petroleum engineer or geologist. Verify production data against state agency records. Assess remaining productive life.
specific_subtasks: Engage qualified reserve engineer (SPE-certified)
Obtain production data from state oil/gas commission (Railroad Commission of Texas, COGCC, etc.)
Commission reserve estimate (SEC-compliant or PRMS methodology)
Review decline curve analysis and projected remaining reserves
Assess well condition and workover/recompletion needs
Verify lease expiration dates and continuous production requirements
Confirm operator financial stability and operational competence

tasks.title: Appraiser Panel Selection
tasks.description: Identify, vet, and engage independent appraisers with appropriate credentials for the asset class. Verify independence, credentials, insurance, and absence of conflicts of interest.
tasks.regulatory_basis: USPAP (Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice) is the recognized standard for professional appraisers in the United States. Using multiple independent appraisers provides institutional-grade valuation confidence and regulatory defensibility.
tasks.regulatory_citation: USPAP Standards Rule 7 (Personal Property); Standards Rule 1-2 (Real Property); ASA Business Valuation Standards

subtasks.title: Identify candidate appraisers (minimum 3)
subtasks.description: Research and identify qualified appraisers with relevant credentials (CGA/MGA for gems, MAI/SRA for real estate, SPE for minerals, ASA for metals).

subtasks.title: Verify credentials and independence
subtasks.description: Confirm active certification/designation, E&O insurance coverage, and no conflicts of interest with any parties to the transaction.

subtasks.title: Execute appraiser engagement letters
subtasks.description: Formalize engagement with each appraiser. Define scope, methodology requirements, timeline, and fee (must NOT be percentage-based per USPAP Ethics Rule).

tasks.title: Formal Independent Appraisal Process
tasks.description: Conduct sequential independent appraisals with sealed prior values to prevent anchoring bias. Each appraiser works independently. Minimum 2 appraisals required; 3 recommended for assets >$5M.
tasks.regulatory_basis: The sequential sealed-value methodology prevents anchoring bias and ensures independent valuation. USPAP Ethics Rule prohibits percentage-based fees. Multiple independent appraisals exceed industry standard (1-2) and provide institutional-grade confidence.
tasks.regulatory_citation: USPAP Ethics Rule; USPAP Standards Rules 7-8 (Personal Property) or 1-2 (Real Property)

gemstones.methodology: Sequential 3-appraisal process. Stone moves physically between appraisers. Each works with sealed prior values. USPAP Standards Rule 7 (Personal Property).
specific_subtasks: Arrange insured transport from vault to Appraiser 1
Appraiser 1 completes USPAP-compliant appraisal
Seal Appraiser 1 value; transport stone to Appraiser 2
Appraiser 2 completes independent appraisal
Seal Appraiser 2 value; transport stone to Appraiser 3
Appraiser 3 completes independent appraisal
Return stone to vault with chain-of-custody documentation

precious_metals.methodology: For standard bullion, market value is straightforward (weight x spot price x purity). For numismatic items, minimum 2 independent appraisals required.
specific_subtasks: For bullion: calculate market value from assay weight, purity, and LBMA fix
For numismatic: engage 2-3 independent numismatic appraisers
Verify premium-over-spot calculations for rare items
Document methodology and market data sources

real_estate.methodology: Minimum 2 MAI-designated appraisers. Income approach (DCF), sales comparison, and cost approach per USPAP Standards Rules 1-2.
specific_subtasks: Appraiser 1 conducts site inspection and market research
Appraiser 1 delivers USPAP-compliant appraisal (all 3 approaches)
Appraiser 2 conducts independent inspection and analysis
Appraiser 2 delivers USPAP-compliant appraisal
Optional: Appraiser 3 for assets >$10M
Review and compare all approaches (income, sales comparison, cost)

mineral_rights.methodology: Reserve-based valuation using SEC-compliant methodology. PV-10 (present value at 10% discount) is industry standard. Income approach based on projected production and commodity prices.
specific_subtasks: Reserve engineer completes independent reserve estimate
Calculate PV-10 using SEC pricing (12-month average) and NYMEX strip pricing
Apply appropriate risk factors for reserve category (proved, probable, possible)
Engage second independent evaluator for cross-check
Compare reserve-based valuation with comparable mineral rights transactions

tasks.title: Variance Analysis & Offering Value Determination
tasks.description: Compare all independent appraisals. If variance exceeds 15-20%, trigger review or additional appraisal. Offering value is determined conservatively — typically the average of the two lowest appraisals.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Variance analysis is a statistical due diligence control. Conservative valuation methodology (not using the highest appraisal) demonstrates investor protection and regulatory defensibility. Required for PPM disclosure accuracy.

subtasks.title: Compile appraisal comparison matrix
subtasks.description: Create side-by-side comparison of all appraisals: values, methodologies used, assumptions, and key differences.

subtasks.title: Calculate variance percentage
subtasks.description: Compute variance between highest and lowest appraisals. Flag if exceeds 15-20% threshold.

subtasks.title: Trigger resolution process if variance exceeds threshold
subtasks.description: If variance is excessive: request appraiser clarification, commission 4th appraisal, or document material reasons for divergence.

subtasks.title: Determine and lock offering value
subtasks.description: Set offering value using conservative methodology (average of two lowest). Document rationale. Lock value — no changes without re-appraisal.

subtasks.title: CEO + CRO sign-off on offering value
subtasks.description: Formal sign-off on the offering value that will appear in the PPM.

tasks.title: Environmental & Regulatory Due Diligence (Asset-Specific)
tasks.description: Conduct any additional regulatory due diligence specific to the asset class that was not covered in the certification and appraisal stages.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Asset-specific regulatory requirements vary by class. Failure to identify regulatory barriers before structuring the offering wastes significant legal and compliance expenditure.

gemstones.description: Verify compliance with Kimberley Process (diamonds). Check import/export restrictions. Verify no cultural heritage claims (antiquities).
specific_subtasks: Confirm Kimberley Process Certificate validity (diamonds)
Verify no CITES restrictions (certain coral, tortoiseshell, ivory)
Check for cultural heritage or restitution claims
Verify import documentation and customs records

precious_metals.description: Verify compliance with LBMA Responsible Sourcing requirements. Check conflict minerals regulations. Verify no FinCEN suspicious activity.
specific_subtasks: Verify LBMA Responsible Sourcing compliance for gold/silver
Check Dodd-Frank Section 1502 conflict minerals compliance
Verify no FinCEN Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) history
Confirm no IRS seizure risk (structured transactions)

real_estate.description: Complete environmental due diligence. Verify zoning and entitlements. Check for rent control, historic preservation, or FIRPTA implications.
specific_subtasks: Review Phase I ESA results; commission Phase II if indicated
Verify zoning compliance and entitlements
Check for rent control or tenant protection ordinances
Assess FIRPTA (Foreign Investment in Real Property Tax Act) implications
Review any pending eminent domain or condemnation actions
Verify compliance with ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act)
Check flood zone designation (FEMA maps) and insurance requirements

mineral_rights.description: Verify environmental compliance, well plugging obligations, and regulatory permits. Check for state-specific bonding requirements.
specific_subtasks: Verify all environmental permits are current (EPA, state agencies)
Assess orphan well and plugging liability obligations
Review bond or financial assurance requirements
Check for any outstanding regulatory violations or NOVs
Verify NORM (Naturally Occurring Radioactive Material) compliance
Review water disposal and injection well permits
Assess regulatory risk of changing environmental regulations

stages.description: Secure the asset in institutional custody, obtain adequate insurance coverage, and prepare the asset for the securities offering structure. This stage ensures the physical asset is protected, verified, and legally ready for investor capital.

gate_at_end.gate_description: Asset in institutional custody. Insurance verified at offering value. Custody receipt issued. Proof of Reserve (if tokenization path) configured. All custody agreements executed.
required_to_pass: Asset physically in institutional custody (or title recorded to SPV for real estate)
Insurance coverage at or above offering value
Custody receipt or title deed issued
All custody/insurance agreements fully executed
Proof of Reserve oracle configured (tokenization path only)

tasks.title: Custody Facility Selection & Agreement
tasks.description: Select an institutional-grade custody facility appropriate for the asset class. Execute custody agreement with segregated storage provisions. Ensure facility can support Proof of Reserve verification.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Institutional custody is required for investor protection and regulatory compliance. Segregated storage ensures the asset is not commingled with other assets. SEC Rule 15c3-3 requires certain custody standards for securities-related assets.
tasks.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 15c3-3 (Customer Protection); UCC 9-313 (perfection by possession)

gemstones.requirements: Segregated safe deposit. Bonded and insured facility. API access for Proof of Reserve. Camera/sensor monitoring. Dual-control access.
specific_subtasks: Request proposals from 2-3 institutional vault providers
Evaluate facility security, insurance, API capability, and costs
Negotiate custody agreement terms
Execute custody agreement with segregated storage provision
Arrange insured transport from current location to vault
Verify segregated allocation and obtain custody receipt
Test API access for Proof of Reserve integration (if tokenization path)

precious_metals.requirements: LBMA-approved vault for Good Delivery bars. Segregated (not pooled) allocation. Regular independent audit. API access.
specific_subtasks: Select LBMA-approved vault facility
Negotiate allocated (not unallocated) storage agreement
Execute custody agreement with segregated allocation
Arrange bonded courier transport (Brink's, Loomis, Malca-Amit)
Verify bar serial numbers upon vault receipt
Obtain vault custody receipt with bar list
Arrange independent annual audit of holdings

real_estate.requirements: Title held by SPV. Title insurance policy. Recorded deed. No physical vault needed — custody is via legal title.
specific_subtasks: Engage title company for closing/recording services
Transfer deed to SPV (or confirm SPV already holds title)
Obtain owner's title insurance policy at offering value
Record deed with county recorder's office
Verify no intervening liens were filed between title search and recording
Obtain recorded deed and title policy copies
If existing property: assign property management agreement to SPV

mineral_rights.requirements: Mineral deed recorded with county. Title opinion updated post-transfer. No physical custody needed — rights are documented via deed.
specific_subtasks: Prepare mineral deed conveying rights to SPV
Execute mineral deed (grantor signature + notarization)
Record mineral deed with county recorder
Obtain updated title opinion reflecting SPV ownership
Notify operator of ownership transfer and update division orders
Update royalty payment instructions to SPV bank account
File assignments of any associated leases or contracts

tasks.title: Insurance Coverage & Verification
tasks.description: Obtain and verify insurance coverage at or above the offering value. Coverage must protect against loss, theft, damage, and (for real estate) liability. SPV must be named as insured or additional insured.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Insurance protects investor capital against physical loss. Coverage certificates naming the SPV as insured are standard institutional practice and may be required by the PPM and custody agreements.
tasks.cost: Varies by asset class and value (typically 0.1-2% of insured value annually)

specific_subtasks: Verify vault specie insurance covers full offering value
Confirm transit insurance for all movements (appraisal chain, vault transfers)
Obtain certificate of insurance naming SPV as additional insured
Verify policy covers theft, loss, damage, and mysterious disappearance
Confirm deductible amounts and exclusions
Verify LBMA vault insurance covers full allocated value
Confirm transit insurance for all physical movements
Obtain certificate of insurance naming SPV as additional insured
Verify coverage for full replacement value (not market value at time of loss)
Obtain property/hazard insurance at replacement cost value
Obtain general liability insurance ($1M-$5M per occurrence)
Obtain flood insurance if property is in FEMA-designated flood zone
Obtain earthquake insurance if in seismic zone (California, etc.)
Verify title insurance policy covers offering value
Obtain umbrella/excess liability policy
Name SPV as named insured on all policies
Verify all lender requirements are met (if existing mortgage)
Verify operator carries adequate OEE and well control insurance
Obtain environmental liability coverage for potential contamination
Consider mineral title insurance for high-value rights
Verify coverage for well blowout, pollution, and cleanup
Confirm SPV is named as additional insured on operator policies

tasks.title: Proof of Reserve / Asset Verification Oracle Setup
tasks.description: Configure cryptographic or independent verification mechanism that allows investors and smart contracts to verify the asset exists and is in custody. Required for tokenization path; recommended for all paths.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Proof of Reserve provides cryptographic verification that physical assets backing the tokens exist in custody. This is an investor protection mechanism enabling real-time verification without trusting the issuer alone.

asset_class_variations.gemstones: Chainlink PoR oracle connected to vault API. Custom external adapter translates vault inventory data into on-chain attestation. Testnet verification before mainnet.
asset_class_variations.precious_metals: Chainlink PoR oracle connected to LBMA vault reporting or custodian API. Can leverage existing LBMA audit framework. Cross-reference with independent audit reports.
asset_class_variations.real_estate: On-chain attestation linked to recorded deed, title insurance, and property management reporting. Less automated — may rely on periodic manual attestation by auditor or title company.
asset_class_variations.mineral_rights: On-chain attestation linked to state production reporting APIs, operator reports, and independent reserve estimates. Can automate production data feeds from state agencies.

subtasks.title: Design PoR architecture for asset class
subtasks.description: Determine data sources, oracle configuration, update frequency, and fallback mechanisms.

subtasks.title: Develop custom external adapter (if needed)
subtasks.description: Build adapter to translate custodian/vault/registry API data into Chainlink-compatible format.

subtasks.title: Deploy and test on testnet
subtasks.description: Deploy PoR feed on testnet. Test all scenarios: normal operation, API failure, custody change, emergency pause.

subtasks.title: Verify oracle-gated minting blocks unauthorized tokens
subtasks.description: Confirm that tokens cannot be minted if PoR verification fails.

stages.description: Form the SPV, draft all legal documents, select the regulatory exemption, prepare SEC filings, and obtain all legal and compliance sign-offs. This is the most document-intensive stage.

gate_at_end.gate_description: SPV formed. PPM finalized. All legal documents reviewed by securities counsel. Compliance sign-off obtained. SEC filing prepared (not yet filed). Blue sky strategy determined.
required_to_pass: SPV (Series LLC) formed with EIN and bank account
PPM, Subscription Agreement, and Investor Questionnaire finalized
Securities counsel opinion letter obtained
Compliance officer sign-off on all offering documents
Form D or Form 1-A prepared and ready for filing
Blue sky filing strategy documented

tasks.title: SPV Formation
tasks.description: Form a dedicated Special Purpose Vehicle (typically a Series LLC) to hold the asset and issue securities. The SPV isolates liability from the parent company and from other assets.
tasks.regulatory_basis: SPV formation is required to isolate asset risk from the parent company and from other assets. Series LLC structure (typically Wyoming or Delaware) allows multiple series with separate liability without forming separate entities.
tasks.regulatory_citation: Wyoming LLC Act 17-29-211 (Series LLC); Delaware LLC Act 6 Del. C. 18-215

subtasks.title: Select formation jurisdiction
subtasks.description: Choose Wyoming (Series LLC favorable) or Delaware (most established LLC law) based on counsel recommendation and asset location.

subtasks.title: File articles of organization
subtasks.description: Prepare and file articles with the Secretary of State. Include series provisions if using Series LLC structure.

subtasks.title: Draft operating agreement
subtasks.description: Draft comprehensive operating agreement covering: management authority, capital contributions, profit distributions (waterfall), investor rights, transfer restrictions, dissolution, and regulatory compliance provisions.

subtasks.title: Obtain EIN from IRS
subtasks.description: Apply for Employer Identification Number (EIN) via IRS Form SS-4 or online application.

subtasks.title: Open SPV bank account
subtasks.description: Open dedicated bank account for the SPV at a regulated financial institution. Wire and ACH capabilities required.

subtasks.title: Appoint registered agent
subtasks.description: Engage registered agent in the formation state for service of process and annual report filings.

subtasks.title: Execute board/manager resolutions
subtasks.description: Adopt resolutions authorizing the securities offering, appointment of officers, engagement of service providers, and bank account opening.

tasks.title: Regulatory Exemption Selection
tasks.description: Determine the optimal SEC exemption for the offering based on target investor base, maximum raise amount, general solicitation needs, and ongoing reporting tolerance.
tasks.regulatory_basis: All securities offerings must either be registered with the SEC or qualify for an exemption. The choice of exemption determines investor eligibility, marketing restrictions, filing requirements, and ongoing obligations.
tasks.regulatory_citation: Securities Act of 1933, Sections 3-5; Regulation D (17 CFR 230.500-508); Regulation A (17 CFR 230.251-263); Regulation S (17 CFR 230.901-905); Regulation CF (17 CFR 227)

subtasks.title: Analyze exemption options
subtasks.description: Compare Reg D 506(b), Reg D 506(c), Reg A+ Tier 1, Reg A+ Tier 2, Reg CF, and Reg S based on deal parameters.

subtasks.title: Document exemption selection rationale
subtasks.description: Prepare memo documenting chosen exemption, rationale, limitations, and compliance requirements.

subtasks.title: Counsel sign-off on exemption choice
subtasks.description: Securities counsel confirms the selected exemption is appropriate and defensible.

reg_d_506b.description: Private placement. No general solicitation. Up to 35 non-accredited investors (sophisticated). Unlimited accredited. No SEC qualification needed.
reg_d_506b.sec_filing: Form D within 15 days of first sale

reg_d_506c.description: Private placement with general solicitation. All purchasers must be accredited. Issuer must take 'reasonable steps' to verify accreditation.
reg_d_506c.sec_filing: Form D within 15 days of first sale
verification_methods: Review tax returns (2 most recent years showing income >$200K/$300K joint)
Review bank/brokerage statements showing net worth >$1M
Written confirmation from CPA, attorney, RIA, or broker-dealer
Third-party verification service (Verify Investor, Parallel Markets, etc.)
For investments >$200K: minimum investment amount may serve as verification factor

reg_a_plus_tier_1.description: Mini-IPO. Both accredited and non-accredited. State blue sky qualification required.
reg_a_plus_tier_1.ongoing_reporting: None at SEC level (state reporting may apply)

reg_a_plus_tier_2.description: Larger mini-IPO. Both accredited and non-accredited. State preempted. Annual/semi-annual reporting required.
reg_a_plus_tier_2.investor_type: All investors (non-accredited limited to 10% of income/net worth per offering)

reg_s.description: Offshore offering to non-US persons. No SEC registration. Can be combined with Reg D for dual US/international offering.

reg_cf.description: Crowdfunding via registered intermediary (funding portal or broker-dealer). Low barrier.
reg_cf.investor_type: All investors (investment limits based on income/net worth)

tasks.title: PPM & Offering Document Drafting
tasks.description: Securities counsel drafts the Private Placement Memorandum (PPM), Subscription Agreement, Investor Questionnaire, and all path-specific offering documents. This is the primary disclosure document for the offering.
tasks.regulatory_basis: The PPM is the primary disclosure document for Regulation D offerings. It must disclose all material risks, conflicts of interest, fee structures, and use of proceeds. Inadequate disclosure exposes the issuer to Section 12(a)(2) rescission liability and potential SEC enforcement.
tasks.regulatory_citation: Securities Act Section 12(a)(2), 15 U.S.C. 77l; SEC Rule 502(b); SEC Rule 10b-5 (anti-fraud)

subtasks.title: Draft PPM (Private Placement Memorandum)
subtasks.description: Comprehensive disclosure document including: executive summary, risk factors (min 20-30 specific risks), use of proceeds, fee structure, management team, asset description, valuation methodology, custody arrangements, exit strategy, and subscription instructions.
ppm_sections: Cover page and table of contents
Risk Factors (asset-specific, market, regulatory, operational, liquidity, technology)
Description of the Asset (based on certifications and appraisals)
Tax Considerations (K-1 distribution, depreciation, depletion for minerals)

subtasks.title: Draft Subscription Agreement
subtasks.description: Investor subscription document including: investor representations, accreditation certification, investment amount, signature blocks, wire instructions, and suitability acknowledgments.

subtasks.title: Draft Investor Questionnaire
subtasks.description: Accredited investor verification questionnaire. Collects income, net worth, investment experience, and accreditation basis per SEC standards.

subtasks.title: Draft path-specific supplemental documents
subtasks.description: Tokenization: Token Purchase Agreement, Wallet Requirements. Fractional: Unit Purchase Agreement. Debt: Promissory Note, Security Agreement, Guaranty.

subtasks.title: Internal compliance review of all documents
subtasks.description: Compliance officer reviews all offering documents for accuracy, completeness, and regulatory compliance.

subtasks.title: Securities counsel final review and opinion letter
subtasks.description: Securities counsel conducts final review and issues opinion letter confirming the offering qualifies for the selected exemption.

tasks.title: Transfer Agent & Broker-Dealer Engagement
tasks.description: Engage SEC-registered transfer agent for cap table management and FINRA-registered broker-dealer for primary distribution and/or secondary trading.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Transfer agents maintain the official shareholder registry and must be SEC-registered. Broker-dealers facilitate securities transactions and must be FINRA-registered. ATS operators must file Form ATS with the SEC and comply with Regulation ATS.
tasks.regulatory_citation: Securities Exchange Act Section 17A (transfer agents); Regulation ATS (17 CFR 242.300-303); FINRA Rules 5110-5123 (private placements)

subtasks.title: Select and engage transfer agent
subtasks.description: Choose SEC-registered transfer agent capable of handling the offering structure (tokenized shares, fractional units, or debt participations). Options: Rialto Transfer Services, Vertalo, Securitize, Tokeny.

subtasks.title: Select and engage broker-dealer
subtasks.description: Choose FINRA-registered broker-dealer for primary distribution. Options: Rialto Markets, Dalmore Group, North Capital. Negotiate placement terms.

subtasks.title: FINRA private placement filing (if required)
subtasks.description: Broker-dealer files private placement materials with FINRA per FINRA Rule 5123. Materials include PPM, subscription agreement, and term sheet.

subtasks.title: ATS registration confirmation (for secondary trading)
subtasks.description: If secondary trading is planned: confirm ATS is registered with SEC (Form ATS filed) and FINRA-approved for tokenized securities.

subtasks.title: Negotiate and execute placement agreements
subtasks.description: Finalize terms with BD: placement fee (typically 1-7%), expense reimbursement, exclusivity, and termination provisions.

tasks.title: SEC Filing Preparation
tasks.description: Prepare all SEC filings required for the chosen exemption. This includes Form D (Reg D), Form 1-A (Reg A+), or Form C (Reg CF). Prepare but do NOT file until distribution stage.
tasks.regulatory_basis: SEC filings are required for most securities exemptions. Form D must be filed within 15 calendar days of the first sale. Form 1-A requires SEC qualification before any sales can occur.
tasks.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 503 (Form D filing); Regulation A Rule 251-263 (Form 1-A); 17 CFR 227.203 (Form C)

subtasks.title: Obtain EDGAR access codes (if not already registered)
subtasks.description: Register entity with SEC EDGAR system. Obtain CIK number (48 hours), then generate EDGAR access codes.

subtasks.title: Prepare Form D (Reg D offerings)
subtasks.description: Complete Form D with: issuer information, related persons, offering details (type, amount, exemption claimed), sales compensation, and offering recipients. NOTE: Do not file until first sale.

subtasks.title: Prepare Form 1-A (Reg A+ offerings)
subtasks.description: Complete Form 1-A Parts I-III: notification, offering circular, and exhibits. Submit to SEC for qualification review. Budget 60-90 days for SEC review.

subtasks.title: Prepare Blue Sky filing strategy
subtasks.description: Document state-by-state blue sky filing requirements. For 506(c): notice filing only in states where securities will be sold (46 states require it). For Reg A+ Tier 1: state qualification required.

reg_d_506.description: Covered securities — state registration preempted. Notice filing required in most states.
reg_d_506.filing_deadline: Generally within 15-30 days of first sale in that state
reg_d_506.fees_range: $0-$600 per state (New York up to $1,900 for large offerings)

key_states.new_york: Must file BEFORE first sale to NY residents. Fee: $300-$1,900 based on amount.
key_states.california: File within 15 days of first sale. Fee: $300 (offerings <$500K) to $2,500 (>$5M).

tasks.title: AML/BSA Compliance Program Setup
tasks.description: Establish or verify the Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Bank Secrecy Act (BSA) compliance program for the offering entity. This includes written AML policies, designated compliance officer, ongoing monitoring procedures, and SAR/CTR filing protocols.
tasks.regulatory_basis: The Bank Secrecy Act requires financial institutions to establish AML programs. While SPVs may not be directly subject to BSA, broker-dealer partners are, and the issuer should maintain its own AML program as a best practice and to satisfy institutional investor due diligence.
tasks.regulatory_citation: BSA 31 USC 5318(h); FinCEN 31 CFR 1010; USA PATRIOT Act Section 326

subtasks.title: Draft written AML/BSA policy
subtasks.description: Create or update written AML policy covering: customer identification program (CIP), enhanced due diligence (EDD), sanctions screening, suspicious activity monitoring, and record retention.

subtasks.title: Designate AML compliance officer
subtasks.description: Formally designate the person responsible for AML compliance. Document in corporate records.

subtasks.title: Implement ongoing monitoring procedures
subtasks.description: Establish procedures for ongoing transaction monitoring, periodic re-screening against sanctions lists, and suspicious activity escalation protocols.

subtasks.title: Establish SAR/CTR filing procedures
subtasks.description: Document procedures for filing Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) and Currency Transaction Reports (CTRs) if applicable. Train relevant staff.

stages.description: This stage diverges significantly by value path. For tokenization: smart contract development, audit, and deployment. For fractional securities: unit structuring and transfer agent configuration. For debt instruments: loan structuring and UCC filings.

gate_at_end.gate_description: Securities structure deployed and tested. Smart contracts audited (if tokenization). Units configured (if fractional). Loan documents executed (if debt). Ready for investor onboarding.
required_to_pass: Smart contract deployed and audited (tokenization) OR units configured in transfer agent (fractional) OR loan documents executed (debt)
All compliance rules programmed and tested
Testnet/sandbox testing complete with no critical issues
Legal counsel sign-off on technical implementation
PoR oracle verified on mainnet (tokenization)

tokenization.title: Token Platform Configuration
tokenization.description: Configure token parameters on the selected tokenization platform. Set symbol, total supply, compliance rules, jurisdiction restrictions, and wallet whitelisting requirements.

subtasks.title: Select token standard (ERC-3643 or ERC-7518)
subtasks.description: ERC-3643 (T-REX): identity-based compliance, widely adopted. ERC-7518 (Zoniqx): dynamic compliance with on-chain rulesets. Select based on partner capabilities.

subtasks.title: Configure token parameters
subtasks.description: Set: token name, symbol, total supply, decimals, and metadata URI. Map to SPV legal structure.

subtasks.title: Define compliance rule set
subtasks.description: Program: accredited investor whitelist, jurisdiction blocks (OFAC countries), transfer restrictions, holding period enforcement (Rule 144 — 12 months), and maximum holder limits.

tokenization.title: Smart Contract Development
tokenization.description: Develop or configure the security token smart contract with embedded compliance rules. Includes identity registry, compliance module, token contract, and oracle integration.

subtasks.title: Develop identity registry contract
subtasks.description: Whitelist contract storing verified investor wallet addresses. Only whitelisted addresses can hold or receive tokens.

subtasks.title: Develop compliance module
subtasks.description: On-chain rules engine enforcing: accreditation verification, jurisdiction restrictions, transfer limits, holding periods, and maximum investor count.

subtasks.title: Integrate Chainlink PoR oracle
subtasks.description: Connect PoR feed to minting function. Tokens can only be minted when oracle confirms reserves are verified.

subtasks.title: Implement pause and emergency functions
subtasks.description: Admin functions for emergency pause, token freeze (per address), and clawback (regulatory compliance requirement).

tokenization.title: Testnet Deployment & Testing
tokenization.description: Deploy all contracts to Polygon testnet (Amoy). Comprehensive testing of all functions, compliance rules, edge cases, and oracle integration.

subtasks.title: Deploy contracts to testnet
subtasks.description: Deploy identity registry, compliance module, token contract, and PoR integration to Polygon Amoy testnet.

subtasks.title: Test compliance rules
subtasks.description: Test: non-whitelisted transfer blocked, sanctioned jurisdiction blocked, holding period enforced, maximum holder count enforced.

subtasks.title: Test oracle-gated minting
subtasks.description: Test: minting succeeds when PoR confirms reserves, minting fails when PoR is stale or negative.

subtasks.title: Test emergency functions
subtasks.description: Test: pause halts all transfers, freeze locks specific address, clawback returns tokens to treasury.

subtasks.title: Document test results
subtasks.description: Compile test report with all scenarios tested, results, and any issues found.

tokenization.title: Smart Contract Security Audit
tokenization.description: Independent security audit of all deployed contracts by a recognized audit firm. Remediate all findings before mainnet deployment.

subtasks.title: Engage audit firm
subtasks.description: Select recognized audit firm (Trail of Bits, OpenZeppelin, Certik, Hacken, etc.). Provide codebase and documentation.

subtasks.title: Conduct audit
subtasks.description: Audit firm reviews all contracts for: reentrancy, access control, integer overflow, logic errors, gas optimization, and compliance gaps.

subtasks.title: Remediate findings
subtasks.description: Fix all critical, high, and medium severity findings. Document mitigation for any accepted risks.

subtasks.title: Obtain clean audit report
subtasks.description: Receive final audit report confirming all critical issues resolved. This is a PPM disclosure item.

tokenization.title: Mainnet Deployment
tokenization.description: Deploy security token contracts to Polygon mainnet. Final configuration review by counsel. Activate PoR oracle. Token contract is live and immutable.

subtasks.title: Final pre-deployment review
subtasks.description: CTO and legal counsel review all contract parameters match PPM disclosure. Verify gas settings and deployment wallet security.

subtasks.title: Deploy to Polygon mainnet
subtasks.description: Deploy all contracts using hardware wallet. Verify deployment transactions on Polygonscan.

subtasks.title: Activate PoR oracle on mainnet
subtasks.description: Activate Chainlink PoR feed. Verify first mainnet attestation is successful.

subtasks.title: Verify contract on block explorer
subtasks.description: Verify and publish contract source code on Polygonscan for transparency.

subtasks.title: Document deployment record
subtasks.description: Record all contract addresses, transaction hashes, deployment parameters, and admin wallet addresses.

fractional_securities.title: Unit Structure Configuration
fractional_securities.description: Define fractional unit parameters: total units, minimum investment, unit price, investor rights, voting provisions, and distribution waterfall.

subtasks.title: Calculate unit structure
subtasks.description: Determine: total units = offering value / unit price. Set minimum investment ($10K-$100K). Define investor rights per unit.

subtasks.title: Define distribution waterfall
subtasks.description: Structure profit/loss distribution: preferred return (if any), return of capital, profit split. Document in operating agreement.

subtasks.title: Configure transfer agent cap table
subtasks.description: Set up cap table in transfer agent system (Vertalo, Carta, etc.) reflecting unit structure, authorized units, and restriction legend.

fractional_securities.title: Transfer Agent Configuration
fractional_securities.description: Configure the SEC-registered transfer agent to manage the cap table, process subscriptions, handle transfers, and maintain the official shareholder registry.

subtasks.title: Configure unit classes and restrictions
subtasks.description: Set up unit classes, transfer restrictions (12-month holding period), and legend requirements in transfer agent system.

subtasks.title: Set up subscription processing workflow
subtasks.description: Configure automated subscription acceptance: document collection, accreditation verification, payment confirmation, unit issuance.

subtasks.title: Test end-to-end subscription flow
subtasks.description: Process test subscription through entire workflow: application, verification, payment, issuance, cap table update.

debt_instruments.title: Loan Terms Structuring
debt_instruments.description: Define loan parameters: principal, interest rate (12-18% APR for alternative lending), LTV ratio (50-70%), term (12-36 months), payment schedule, covenants, and default triggers.

subtasks.title: Structure loan terms
subtasks.description: Define: principal amount, interest rate, LTV ratio, term, amortization schedule, prepayment provisions, covenants, and default triggers.

subtasks.title: Draft promissory note
subtasks.description: Legal drafting of promissory note with all terms, payment schedule, default provisions, and remedies.

subtasks.title: Draft security agreement
subtasks.description: Grant security interest in the asset to lender. Define collateral description, perfection methods, and foreclosure procedures.

subtasks.title: Draft personal guaranty (if applicable)
subtasks.description: If borrower is an entity: draft personal guaranty from principals if required by lender.

debt_instruments.title: UCC Filing & Security Interest Perfection
debt_instruments.description: Perfect the security interest in the collateral via UCC Article 9 filing and/or possession. File UCC-1 financing statement with the appropriate Secretary of State.
debt_instruments.regulatory_basis: UCC Article 9 governs secured transactions in personal property. For tangible assets (gems, metals), perfection requires filing + possession. For mineral rights (as-extracted collateral), file in the state where the minerals are located. New UCC Article 12 (adopted in 30+ states by 2026) governs controllable electronic records including security tokens.
debt_instruments.regulatory_citation: UCC 9-310 (filing); UCC 9-313 (possession); UCC 9-102(a)(6) (as-extracted collateral); UCC Article 12 (controllable electronic records)

subtasks.title: Prepare UCC-1 financing statement
subtasks.description: Complete UCC-1 form with: debtor name/address (exact legal name), secured party, and collateral description.

subtasks.title: File UCC-1 with Secretary of State
subtasks.description: File in debtor's state of organization (entities) or state of residence (individuals). For as-extracted collateral (minerals): file in state where minerals are located + record with county.

subtasks.title: Obtain filed UCC-1 confirmation
subtasks.description: Receive filed copy with filing number and timestamp from Secretary of State.

subtasks.title: Verify priority (lien search)
subtasks.description: Run UCC lien search to verify no prior security interests in the same collateral. Address any existing liens.

subtasks.title: Perfect by possession (tangible assets)
subtasks.description: For gems/metals: vault custodian holds collateral as bailee for the lender. Execute bailee letter / tripartite custody agreement.

debt_instruments.title: Lender Identification & Engagement
debt_instruments.description: Source lending capital from institutional lenders, alternative credit funds, family offices, or syndication partners. Match lender requirements with deal parameters.

subtasks.title: Prepare loan package for lender presentation
subtasks.description: Compile: asset description, appraisal reports, custody verification, borrower financials, proposed terms, and exit strategy.

subtasks.title: Present to target lenders
subtasks.description: Distribute loan package to pre-qualified lenders. Conduct presentations and answer due diligence questions.

subtasks.title: Negotiate final terms
subtasks.description: Negotiate interest rate, LTV, covenants, fees, and other material terms with selected lender.

subtasks.title: Execute commitment letter
subtasks.description: Obtain lender commitment letter with final terms, conditions precedent, and closing timeline.

debt_instruments.title: Collateral Custody Agreement (Tripartite)
debt_instruments.description: Execute three-party agreement between borrower, lender, and vault custodian. Defines release conditions, reappraisal triggers, default procedures, and custodian obligations.

subtasks.title: Draft tripartite custody agreement
subtasks.description: Define: custodian duties, release authorization (lender must consent), reappraisal triggers (if collateral value drops >15%), and default procedures.

subtasks.title: Execute agreement (all three parties)
subtasks.description: Obtain signatures from borrower, lender, and vault custodian.

subtasks.title: Verify lender named as loss payee on insurance
subtasks.description: Confirm insurance certificate names lender as loss payee. Lender must receive direct notice of any coverage changes.

stages.description: File regulatory documents, onboard investors, process subscriptions, issue securities/tokens, and manage the offering through its lifecycle. Includes ongoing compliance, reporting, secondary trading facilitation, and eventual exit/liquidation.

gate_at_end.gate_description: SEC filing submitted. First subscription processed and funded. Cap table/token ledger active. Ongoing reporting operational.
required_to_pass: Form D filed with SEC (within 15 days of first sale)
Blue sky filings submitted in applicable states
At least one subscription processed and funded
Cap table or token ledger updated
Ongoing reporting schedule established

tasks.title: SEC Filing Submission
tasks.description: File Form D (Reg D) or Form 1-A (Reg A+) with the SEC via EDGAR. For Reg D: file within 15 calendar days of first sale. For Reg A+: file before any sales and await qualification.
tasks.estimated_duration: 1-2 days (Reg D) or 60-90 days (Reg A+ qualification)
tasks.regulatory_basis: SEC Rule 503 requires Form D filing within 15 calendar days of first sale for Reg D offerings. Regulation A requires SEC qualification of Form 1-A before any sales. Failure to file does not void the exemption for Reg D but may result in SEC enforcement action.
tasks.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 503 (17 CFR 230.503); Regulation A Rule 252 (17 CFR 230.252)

subtasks.title: File Form D via EDGAR (Reg D)
subtasks.description: Submit completed Form D through SEC EDGAR system. Confirm successful filing and retain confirmation.

subtasks.title: Submit Form 1-A via EDGAR (Reg A+)
subtasks.description: Submit Form 1-A with offering circular and exhibits. Respond to SEC staff comments during qualification review.

subtasks.title: File Form D amendments (as needed)
subtasks.description: File amendments annually if offering continues, or when material changes occur (e.g., offering amount increase, new executive officers).

tasks.title: Blue Sky State Filings
tasks.description: Submit state securities notice filings in all jurisdictions where securities will be sold. Required even for Reg D 506(c) offerings despite federal preemption.
tasks.regulatory_basis: While Reg D 506 offerings are federal 'covered securities' preempted from state registration, 46 states still require notice filings and fees. New York requires pre-filing before first sale to NY residents.
tasks.regulatory_citation: NSMIA Section 18 (15 U.S.C. 77r); Individual state securities statutes

subtasks.title: Determine filing jurisdictions
subtasks.description: Identify all states where securities will be offered or sold. Include state of incorporation and states where investors reside.

subtasks.title: Prepare state notice filings
subtasks.description: Complete each state's notice filing form. Attach Form D, PPM cover page, and required fees.

subtasks.title: Submit filings (New York FIRST — pre-sale requirement)
subtasks.description: Submit New York filing before any sales to NY residents. Submit all other states within 15-30 days of first sale.

subtasks.title: Track and confirm all filings
subtasks.description: Maintain filing log with: state, date filed, fee paid, confirmation number, and renewal date.

tasks.title: Investor Marketing & Outreach
tasks.description: Execute investor marketing campaign within the boundaries of the chosen exemption. For 506(c): general solicitation is permitted. For 506(b): no general solicitation — pre-existing relationships only.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Reg D 506(c) permits general solicitation and advertising. Reg D 506(b) prohibits general solicitation — all investors must come from pre-existing substantive relationships. Marketing materials must be consistent with PPM disclosures.
tasks.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 506(c)(2); SEC Rule 502(c) (general solicitation prohibition for 506(b)); SEC Rule 156 (investment company advertising)

subtasks.title: Develop investor marketing materials
subtasks.description: Create pitch deck, one-pager, website content, and digital marketing assets. All must be consistent with PPM risk disclosures.

subtasks.title: Compliance review of all marketing materials
subtasks.description: Securities counsel and compliance officer review all marketing materials for accuracy, risk disclosure adequacy, and exemption compliance.

subtasks.title: Execute outreach campaign
subtasks.description: Targeted outreach via: broker-dealer networks, accredited investor platforms (AngelList, Republic, etc.), industry events, digital advertising (506(c) only), and referral networks.

subtasks.title: Track investor pipeline and interest
subtasks.description: Maintain CRM log of all investor contacts, interest level, qualification status, and follow-up actions.

tasks.title: Investor KYC & Accreditation Verification
tasks.description: Each investor completes KYC identity verification, sanctions screening, and accredited investor verification. For 506(c): issuer must take 'reasonable steps' to verify accreditation — self-certification alone is insufficient.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Rule 506(c)(2)(ii) requires issuers to take 'reasonable steps to verify' accredited investor status. The SEC has provided non-exclusive safe harbor verification methods. FinCEN CIP rules require identity verification for all financial transactions.
tasks.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 506(c)(2)(ii) (17 CFR 230.506(c)(2)(ii)); SEC guidance on verification methods; FinCEN 31 CFR 1010.220 (CIP)

subtasks.title: Collect investor identity documents
subtasks.description: Government-issued photo ID, proof of address. For entities: formation documents, beneficial ownership information.

subtasks.title: Run KYC/AML screening
subtasks.description: Screen against OFAC/SDN, PEP databases, adverse media, and global sanctions lists.

subtasks.title: Verify accredited investor status
subtasks.description: Apply SEC safe harbor methods: review tax returns (income >$200K/$300K joint for 2 years), bank/brokerage statements (net worth >$1M excluding primary residence), or obtain third-party verification letter from CPA/attorney/RIA/BD.

subtasks.title: Issue accreditation clearance
subtasks.description: Compliance officer confirms investor passes KYC and accreditation. Investor is cleared to subscribe.

tasks.title: Subscription Processing
tasks.description: Investor signs subscription agreement, wires funds, and receives securities (tokens, units, or note participations). Compliance review on each subscription before acceptance.

subtasks.title: Investor completes subscription agreement
subtasks.description: Investor signs subscription agreement and investor questionnaire. Includes representations, risk acknowledgments, and accreditation certification.

subtasks.title: Investor wires subscription funds
subtasks.description: Investor wires investment amount to SPV bank account. Obtain wire receipt and bank confirmation.

subtasks.title: Compliance review of subscription
subtasks.description: Compliance officer reviews: subscription completeness, accreditation verification, suitability, investment amount within limits, and no red flags.

subtasks.title: Accept or reject subscription
subtasks.description: Issue formal acceptance or rejection. If rejected: return funds within required timeframe.

subtasks.title: Issue securities (tokens, units, or note participations)
subtasks.description: Tokenization: mint tokens to investor's whitelisted wallet. Fractional: transfer agent issues units. Debt: issue participation certificates.

subtasks.title: Update cap table / token ledger
subtasks.description: Record new investor in cap table. Reconcile on-chain and off-chain records (if tokenized).

subtasks.title: Send investor confirmation
subtasks.description: Send written confirmation of investment including: amount, units/tokens received, wallet address (if tokenized), and next steps.

tasks.title: Ongoing Reporting & Compliance
tasks.description: Maintain ongoing reporting obligations, compliance monitoring, and investor communications throughout the offering lifecycle.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Reg D offerings have limited federal reporting requirements but may have contractual obligations per the PPM. Reg A+ Tier 2 requires annual (1-K), semi-annual (1-SA), and current (1-U) reports. All offerings require Form D amendments annually.
tasks.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 503 (Form D amendments); Regulation A Rules 257-259 (ongoing reporting); IRC Section 6031 (partnership returns)

subtasks.title: Quarterly NAV / valuation updates
subtasks.description: Prepare and distribute quarterly Net Asset Value updates to investors. Include market conditions, asset status, and any material developments.

subtasks.title: Annual independent re-appraisal
subtasks.description: Commission annual independent appraisal to verify/update asset value. Required for NAV accuracy and insurance adequacy.

subtasks.title: Annual K-1 tax distribution
subtasks.description: Prepare and distribute Schedule K-1 to all investors by March 15 (or extended deadline). SPV files Form 1065 (partnership return).

subtasks.title: Form D annual amendment
subtasks.description: File Form D amendment with SEC if offering continues past 12 months. Update total amount sold and investor count.

subtasks.title: Blue sky renewals
subtasks.description: Renew state blue sky notice filings as required (varies by state — some annual, some upon amendment).

subtasks.title: Compliance monitoring and audit
subtasks.description: Annual compliance audit: verify all investor records, accreditation documentation, AML screening, and filing status are current.

subtasks.title: Custody verification
subtasks.description: Verify asset remains in custody: inspect vault records, PoR oracle attestation (if tokenized), or title verification (real estate).

subtasks.title: Investor communications
subtasks.description: Regular investor updates: market commentary, asset updates, distribution notices, and material event notifications.

tasks.title: Secondary Transfer Facilitation
tasks.description: Facilitate compliant secondary transfers between investors via ATS (if available) or manual transfer agent processing. All transfers must comply with holding period restrictions and exemption requirements.
tasks.regulatory_basis: Rule 144 imposes a 12-month holding period for securities acquired in Reg D offerings. After the holding period, transfers must still comply with securities laws. ATS trading requires compliance checks on every trade.
tasks.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 144 (17 CFR 230.144); Regulation ATS (17 CFR 242.300-303); Securities Act Section 4(a)(1) (ordinary trading); Section 4(a)(1-1/2) (section 4(a)(7) exemption)

subtasks.title: Verify holding period compliance (12 months for Reg D)
subtasks.description: Confirm seller has held securities for minimum holding period. For tokenized securities: enforced automatically by smart contract.

subtasks.title: Verify buyer accreditation (if required)
subtasks.description: Confirm buyer meets accreditation requirements. For ERC-3643 tokens: verified by on-chain identity registry.

subtasks.title: Process transfer
subtasks.description: Tokenization: buyer/seller trade via ATS with automatic compliance checks. Fractional: transfer agent processes unit transfer. Debt: assignment of participation interest.

subtasks.title: Update cap table / token ledger
subtasks.description: Record transfer in official registry. Reconcile on-chain and off-chain records.

tasks.title: Exit / Liquidation / Redemption
tasks.description: Upon asset sale, offering close, loan maturity, or redemption event: distribute proceeds, burn tokens (if applicable), dissolve SPV, and file all final regulatory documents.
tasks.regulatory_basis: SPV dissolution must follow state LLC dissolution procedures. Final K-1 must be distributed. Form D termination should be filed. UCC-3 termination statement required for debt instruments. Token burn removes securities from circulation.
tasks.regulatory_citation: State LLC dissolution statutes; IRC Section 6031 (final return); UCC 9-513 (termination statement)

subtasks.title: Execute asset sale or disposition
subtasks.description: Sell asset at market value. For real estate: standard closing process. For gems/metals: dealer sale or auction. For minerals: lease assignment or mineral deed sale.

subtasks.title: Calculate and distribute proceeds
subtasks.description: Apply distribution waterfall per operating agreement: return of capital, preferred return, profit split. Calculate and withhold any taxes.

subtasks.title: Burn tokens (tokenization path)
subtasks.description: Execute token burn for all outstanding tokens. Verify total supply equals zero on blockchain.

subtasks.title: Cancel units (fractional path)
subtasks.description: Transfer agent cancels all outstanding units. Cap table shows zero outstanding.

subtasks.title: File UCC-3 termination statement (debt path)
subtasks.description: File UCC-3 with Secretary of State to terminate the security interest upon loan payoff.

subtasks.title: Distribute final K-1
subtasks.description: Prepare and distribute final Schedule K-1 to all investors reflecting their share of gains/losses from the disposition.

subtasks.title: File Form D termination / final Form 1-K
subtasks.description: File final SEC documents: Form D termination amendment or final Form 1-K (Reg A+).

subtasks.title: Dissolve SPV
subtasks.description: File articles of dissolution with Secretary of State. Close bank accounts. Cancel registered agent. Retain records per retention schedule (minimum 7 years).

subtasks.title: Final audit and record retention
subtasks.description: Conduct final audit of all records. Archive per regulatory retention requirements (SEC: 7 years; IRS: 7 years; BSA/AML: 5 years).

record_retention.description: All records must be retained per applicable regulatory requirements.
record_retention.sec_records: 7 years (broker-dealer books and records under Rule 17a-4)
record_retention.tax_records: 7 years (IRS general statute of limitations + buffer)
record_retention.aml_records: 5 years from date of creation or date of last transaction (BSA 31 CFR 1010.430)

data_privacy.description: PII must be encrypted at rest and in transit. Comply with applicable state privacy laws.
data_privacy.general: Encrypt SSN, financial data, and government ID numbers using AES-256 or equivalent

audit_trail.description: Every action in the workflow must produce an immutable audit trail entry.
requirements: Action type (create, update, approve, upload, etc.)
Target entity (asset, document, task, investor, etc.)

conflict_of_interest.description: All parties in the workflow must disclose conflicts of interest. Conflicts must be documented and disclosed in the PPM.
conflict_of_interest.regulatory_citation: SEC Rule 10b-5 (anti-fraud); Investment Advisers Act Section 206

mineral_rights.stage_3_custody: $2,000-$10,000/yr (deed recording + insurance — lower since no physical custody)

timeline_summary.description: Typical end-to-end timeline from lead identification to first investor subscription.

debt_path.stage_4: 4-8 weeks (simpler legal structure for pure lending)

securities_act_1933.description: Federal securities registration and exemption framework

key_sections.topic: Rescission liability for material misstatements in offerings
Private placement with general solicitation (accredited only)
Resale of restricted securities (12-month holding period)

securities_exchange_act_1934.description: Secondary market regulation, broker-dealer registration, transfer agents

key_sections.topic: Customer protection / custody requirements for BDs
Dealers in Precious Metals, Stones, or Jewels (DPMS)
Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Rule — beneficial ownership

ucc.description: Uniform Commercial Code — secured transactions and digital assets

key_sections.topic: Definition of as-extracted collateral (oil, gas, minerals)
Commercially reasonable disposition of collateral upon default

key_provisions.topic: Federal preemption of state registration for covered securities

finra_rules.description: FINRA rules applicable to broker-dealers in private placements

key_rules.topic: Public offerings of securities with conflicts of interest
Suitability (legacy) / Reg BI for retail investors

tax_code.description: Internal Revenue Code provisions for pass-through entities and asset-specific deductions

key_sections.topic: Like-kind exchange (real estate only — NOT personal property post-TCJA)
Depreciation recapture on sale of real estate
Foreign investment in Real Property Tax Act

recent_developments_2025_2026.event: SEC issues no-action letter to DTC for tokenization services
recent_developments_2025_2026.impact: Three-year pilot for DTC to tokenize certain custodied securities. Expected launch H2 2026.

recent_developments_2025_2026.event: SEC Divisions jointly issue guidance on tokenized securities
recent_developments_2025_2026.impact: Clarified how existing federal securities laws apply to various tokenization structures. Addresses Article 8 vs Article 12 UCC treatment.
New legal framework for controllable electronic records including security tokens. Perfection by control available.

recent_developments_2025_2026.event: SEC Division of Trading and Markets statement on broker-dealer custody of crypto asset securities
recent_developments_2025_2026.impact: Clarified that broker-dealers can take 'physical possession' of crypto asset securities by maintaining private key controls consistent with industry best practices.

recent_developments_2025_2026.event: Ironlight Markets receives FINRA approval for first US ATS with on-chain atomic settlement
recent_developments_2025_2026.impact: First FINRA-approved ATS supporting atomic settlement for tokenized securities and RWAs.

governance_requirements_mapping.description: Each task in stages 1-4 maps to a governance_requirements row (shared across all paths). Tasks in stages 5-6 may be path-specific.

fields.value_path: NULL for shared stages (1-4); specific path for stage 5-6 tasks
fields.step_number: Use the step_number field from each task
fields.title: Use the title field
fields.source_url: Use the source_url field where provided
fields.gate_id: G1-G6 as defined in each stage

default_tasks_mapping.description: Each subtask maps to a default_tasks row, linked to its parent governance_requirement.

fields.assigned_role: compliance_officer | ceo | cto | cro | coo | legal_counsel

governance_documents_mapping.description: Each required_documents entry maps to a governance_documents row.

fields.document_type: Must match document_type enum in schema
fields.description: What this document proves or contains
fields.is_required: true for required; false for recommended

asset_class_extension.description: The existing schema uses value_path for path-specific variations. For asset class variations, consider adding an asset_class column to governance_requirements or creating asset_class_overrides table.
asset_class_extension.recommendation: Add asset_class enum (gemstones, precious_metals, real_estate, mineral_rights) to the schema. Allow governance_requirements to be filtered by both value_path AND asset_class. Most requirements are shared across classes; only intake/certification/custody steps differ significantly.
